artifacts.summary:
## Overview
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including the proposal to break up the engineering key review into four department key reviews, and the need to simplify the metrics to prevent gaming. The team agreed to try a two-month rotation for the department key reviews and to focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

## Key Discussion Points
### Department Key Reviews
* Proposal to break up the engineering key review into four department key reviews
	+ Reasons: increased visibility, ability to go deeper, increase objectivity, allow Eric to focus on new markets, and shift into a question-asker mode
	+ Proposal: two-month rotation, with one department per month
* Discussion on the potential for gaming the narrow MR rate by producing fewer MRs with engineers at GitLab

### Metrics
* Discussion on the RD Overall vs RD Wider MR rate
	+ Narrow MR rate includes internal MRs, while wider MR rate includes community contributions
	+ Proposal to track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community as a KPI
* Discussion on the potential for gaming the wider MR rate by producing fewer MRs with engineers at GitLab
* Simplification of metrics to prevent gaming

### Security and Quality
* Discussion on the need to get more security work prioritized
* Proposal to focus on other indicators to improve quality and security

### SUS and Bug Discussion
* Discussion on the SUS metric, with a focus on the potential for a temporary jump in SUS above SLOs if the backlog is cleared out
* Discussion on the bug discussion, with a focus on the underlying problem of metrics measurement

### Rate Discussion
* Discussion on the narrow MR rate, with a focus on the potential for a rebound in March

## Decisions Made
* The team will try a two-month rotation for the department key reviews.
* The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
* The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
* The percentage of total MRs that come from the community will be tracked as a KPI.
* The team will work on improving the security metrics to reflect the prioritization of security work.

## Open Questions
* The team needs to be cautious of the potential for gaming the narrow MR rate by producing fewer MRs with engineers at GitLab.
* The team needs to be aware of the potential for a temporary jump in SUS above SLOs if the backlog is cleared out.
* The team needs to be careful not to over-interpret the narrow MR rate as a measure of productivity, as it may not accurately reflect the quality and security of the work.
artifacts.followups:
## Email
Subject: Follow-up on Engineering Key Review Meeting

Dear Team,

This email serves as a follow-up on the Engineering Key Review meeting held on February 18, 2021. We discussed key review metrics, including the proposal to break up the engineering key review into four department key reviews. The team agreed to try a two-month rotation for the department key reviews, with the development team going more frequently if needed.

We also decided to hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward and focus on other indicators to improve quality and security. Additionally, we will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community as a KPI.

To simplify the metrics and prevent gaming, we will work on improving the security metrics to reflect the prioritization of security work. We will also track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community.

Action items:

* Lily and Max will work on transitioning to track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community.
* The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
* We will work on improving the security metrics to reflect the prioritization of security work.

If you have any questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack
"Update on Engineering Key Review: We've decided to try a 2-month rotation for department key reviews, hold the narrow MR rate at 10, and focus on other indicators to improve quality and security. We'll also track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community as a KPI. Let's simplify the metrics and prevent gaming!"
